title: Normalize SHA hardening source packs into a curated starter catalog
description: Why now: SHA has a working control-plane foundation, but it still lacks trustworthy hardening intelligence. This task creates the first curated source-pack catalog so SHA can reason over starter metadata from Microsoft, CIS, NIST, DISA, and CISA/NSA without relying on live scraping or unstable external files. The slice intentionally stops at curated starter packs plus a deterministic catalog manifest; legacy CSV import and larger corpus ingestion are deferred.
objective: Stand up the first reproducible SHA control catalog so future agent, posture, and remediation work can consume a stable curated starter dataset instead of ad hoc source references.
done_when: The repo contains a checked-in `control-packs/` workspace with curated starter pack files and a generated catalog manifest.
Typed Python contract models exist for starter packs, normalized controls, provenance, mappings, and catalog summaries.
A deterministic builder exists that validates curated starter pack JSON files and generates `control-packs/catalog.json` with no network or external file dependency.
Starter normalized packs exist for Microsoft, CIS, NIST, DISA, and CISA/NSA, and each pack exposes the same pinned normalized fields.
Focused tests verify schema validity, pinned starter inventory, deterministic catalog generation, uniqueness constraints, and byte-stable generated catalog output.
spirit_anti: Do not add live scraping, scheduled sync jobs, legacy CSV import, remediation execution, or UI/API work in this task. Keep the slice deterministic, curated, file-backed, and execution-ready.
constraints: Keep this slice fully local and reproducible: no HTTP fetches, no browser automation, and no dependence on files outside the repo at build or test time.
Use Python 3.13.12 with the existing SHA backend toolchain (`uv`, pytest) and keep the implementation under `backend/` plus repo-root `control-packs/` data files.
Typed source-pack contract models in this slice use Pydantic 2 under `backend/app/source_packs/contracts.py`; do not introduce a second validation/modeling stack.
Do not require Go, Node, or a running backend/frontend service for this task's core verification path.
The normalized source-pack filesystem root is fixed to `control-packs/`, with curated pack inputs under `control-packs/packs/` and generated `control-packs/catalog.json` at the root.

Source-of-truth rule: curated starter pack JSON files under `control-packs/packs/` are authoritative checked-in inputs that the builder validates in place and never rewrites. The builder generates only `control-packs/catalog.json`.

Curated starter pack JSON files are validation-only inputs: they must already be trimmed, sorted, deduplicated, and committed in final form. The builder may reject malformed curated inputs but must not auto-normalize and rewrite them.
Curated pack `controls` arrays must already be sorted by `control_id`; unsorted `controls` arrays are validation errors and must fail before any catalog write.

Validation precedence is fixed for curated starter files: canonical ordering and exact field presence are validated semantically, and unsorted `platforms`, `profiles`, or mapping arrays must fail validation rather than being normalized in memory.

The builder must fail hard on missing fields, extra fields, bad enum values, unsorted control arrays, duplicate IDs, whitespace-only required strings, and any malformed curated JSON input. Partial output files are not allowed on failure.

Curated pack filenames are fixed and exhaustive in this slice: `microsoft.windows-security-baseline-starter.json`, `cis.windows-server-l1-starter.json`, `nist.csf-2.0-starter.json`, `disa.windows-server-stig-starter.json`, and `cisa_nsa.linux-ssh-starter.json`. Unexpected extra JSON files under `control-packs/packs/` must fail validation; non-JSON files are ignored.
Missing any one of the five expected curated starter JSON files is a validation error and must fail before any catalog write.

Repo-root discovery is fixed: the builder must resolve the SHA repo root relative to `backend/scripts/build_source_catalog.py`, so invoking from repo root or from `backend/` produces identical input resolution and identical bytes.
The builder command is fixed to `uv run --directory backend python scripts/build_source_catalog.py` from the repo root or `uv run python scripts/build_source_catalog.py` from `backend/`.
Out-of-repo files referenced under `context.references` are non-normative background only and must never be read during implementation or verification for this curated starter slice.
Source family enum is fixed to `microsoft`, `cis`, `nist`, `disa`, and `cisa_nsa` in this slice.

Curated pack canonical metadata is fixed in this slice: Microsoft uses `pack_id=pack.microsoft.windows-security-baseline-starter`, `source_name=Microsoft Windows Security Baseline Starter`, `source_version=starter-2026.04`, `source_url=https://learn.microsoft.com/windows/security/operating-system-security/device-management/windows-security-configuration-framework/windows-security-baselines`; CIS uses `pack_id=pack.cis.windows-server-l1-starter`, `source_name=CIS Windows Server Level 1 Starter`, `source_version=starter-2026.04`, `source_url=https://www.cisecurity.org/cis-benchmarks`; NIST uses `pack_id=pack.nist.csf-2.0-starter`, `source_name=NIST CSF 2.0 Starter`, `source_version=2.0`, `source_url=https://www.nist.gov/cyberframework`; DISA uses `pack_id=pack.disa.windows-server-stig-starter`, `source_name=DISA Windows Server STIG Starter`, `source_version=starter-2026.04`, `source_url=https://public.cyber.mil/stigs/`; CISA/NSA uses `pack_id=pack.cisa_nsa.linux-ssh-starter`, `source_name=CISA NSA Linux SSH Starter`, `source_version=starter-2026.04`, `source_url=https://www.cisa.gov/resources-tools/resources/guidance-securing-remote-access-software-and-services`.

Each normalized pack object must contain exactly these top-level fields: `pack_id`, `source_family`, `source_name`, `source_version`, `generated_at`, `source_url`, `platforms`, `profiles`, `summary`, and `controls`. All ten fields are required in curated starter JSON, and `generated_at` is pinned to `2026-04-18T00:00:00Z` in every curated pack and in the generated catalog.

Each normalized control object must contain exactly these fields: `control_id`, `title`, `platform`, `profiles`, `severity`, `disruption`, `rollback_complexity`, `auto_remediation_candidate`, `reboot_required`, `guidance_summary`, `detection_summary`, `remediation_summary`, `rollback_summary`, `provenance`, and `mappings`. Every field is required in curated starter JSON, including explicit `mappings` arrays and boolean fields.

Free-text content is deterministic in this slice, not editorial. Each pack `summary` must be exactly `Curated starter pack for <source_name>.`. Each control `guidance_summary` must be exactly `Starter guidance for <title>.`; `detection_summary` exactly `Check state for <title>.`; `remediation_summary` exactly `Apply desired state for <title>.`; `rollback_summary` exactly `Rollback desired state for <title>.`; `provenance.notes` exactly `Starter control selected for <source_name>.`.

Severity enum is fixed to `low`, `medium`, `high`, and `critical`; disruption enum is fixed to `transparent`, `minimal`, `moderate`, `significant`, and `disruptive`; rollback complexity enum is fixed to `low`, `medium`, and `high`.

`provenance` is fixed to an object with required fields `source_locator` and `notes`; `mappings` is fixed to required arrays `cis_control_ids`, `nist_csf_ids`, `sp80053_ids`, and `legacy_sha_ids`; mapping-array values are trimmed, duplicates are rejected, and surviving values are written in ascending lexical order.

Canonical array order is fixed for this slice: `platforms` use `windows`, `linux`; `profiles` use `domain_controller`, `endpoint`, `server`. Arrays must be unique and emitted in that semantic order, not generic lexical order.
Duplicate mapping-array values are checked after trim, so values that differ only by surrounding whitespace are still duplicates and must fail.
Curated pack-level `platforms` and `profiles` are not editorial: the builder must validate that each pack's arrays equal the canonical union of its contained controls exactly.

Curated starter control-id prefixes are fixed per pack: Microsoft `control.microsoft-windows-security-baseline-starter.*`; CIS `control.cis-windows-server-l1-starter.*`; NIST `control.nist-csf-2-0-starter.*`; DISA `control.disa-windows-server-stig-starter.*`; CISA/NSA `control.cisa-nsa-linux-ssh-starter.*`.
Slug grammar is fixed for curated controls: lowercase ASCII letters, digits, and hyphens only; runs of non-alphanumeric characters collapse to one hyphen; leading and trailing hyphens are stripped.

Curated starter inventory is fixed in this slice. Microsoft controls: `control.microsoft-windows-security-baseline-starter.password-history`, `control.microsoft-windows-security-baseline-starter.password-min-length`, `control.microsoft-windows-security-baseline-starter.account-lockout-threshold`. CIS controls: `control.cis-windows-server-l1-starter.smb-signing-server-required`, `control.cis-windows-server-l1-starter.disable-smbv1`, `control.cis-windows-server-l1-starter.disable-guest-account`. NIST controls: `control.nist-csf-2-0-starter.asset-config-baseline`, `control.nist-csf-2-0-starter.audit-log-retention`, `control.nist-csf-2-0-starter.remote-access-review`. DISA controls: `control.disa-windows-server-stig-starter.windows-firewall-enabled`, `control.disa-windows-server-stig-starter.rdp-network-level-authentication`, `control.disa-windows-server-stig-starter.smartscreen-reputation-check`. CISA/NSA controls: `control.cisa-nsa-linux-ssh-starter.disable-root-login`, `control.cisa-nsa-linux-ssh-starter.disable-password-authentication`, `control.cisa-nsa-linux-ssh-starter.allowlisted-users`.

Canonical curated control titles are fixed in this slice. Microsoft: `Enforce password history`, `Enforce minimum password length`, `Enforce account lockout threshold`. CIS: `Require SMB signing on servers`, `Disable SMBv1`, `Disable guest account`. NIST: `Maintain baseline configuration inventory`, `Retain security audit logs`, `Review remote access privileges`. DISA: `Enable Windows Firewall`, `Require Network Level Authentication for RDP`, `Enable SmartScreen reputation checks`. CISA/NSA: `Disable SSH root login`, `Disable SSH password authentication`, `Restrict SSH access to allowlisted users`.

Curated starter controls are fixed to these platforms and profiles: Microsoft all `windows` with `[endpoint,server]`; CIS all `windows` with `[server]`; NIST uses `asset-config-baseline -> windows [domain_controller,endpoint,server]`, `audit-log-retention -> linux [server]`, `remote-access-review -> windows [domain_controller,server]`; DISA all `windows` with `[server]`; CISA/NSA all `linux` with `[server]`.

Curated starter controls are fixed to these severity/disruption/rollback bands and booleans: Microsoft `password-history medium/minimal/low/true/false`, `password-min-length high/moderate/medium/true/false`, `account-lockout-threshold high/minimal/low/true/false`; CIS `smb-signing-server-required high/minimal/low/true/false`, `disable-smbv1 high/significant/medium/true/true`, `disable-guest-account medium/minimal/low/true/false`; NIST `asset-config-baseline high/minimal/medium/false/false`, `audit-log-retention medium/minimal/low/false/false`, `remote-access-review high/minimal/low/false/false`; DISA `windows-firewall-enabled high/significant/medium/true/false`, `rdp-network-level-authentication high/minimal/low/true/false`, `smartscreen-reputation-check medium/minimal/low/true/false`; CISA/NSA `disable-root-login critical/significant/medium/true/false`, `disable-password-authentication critical/significant/high/true/false`, `allowlisted-users high/moderate/medium/true/false`.

Curated starter controls use pinned mapping arrays: Microsoft `password-history -> cis_control_ids [4.1], legacy_sha_ids [SHA001]`; `password-min-length -> cis_control_ids [4.1], legacy_sha_ids [SHA004]`; `account-lockout-threshold -> cis_control_ids [4.1], legacy_sha_ids [SHA009]`. CIS `smb-signing-server-required -> cis_control_ids [4.1], legacy_sha_ids [SHA027]`; `disable-smbv1 -> cis_control_ids [4.1], legacy_sha_ids []`; `disable-guest-account -> cis_control_ids [4.1], legacy_sha_ids [SHA013]`. NIST `asset-config-baseline -> nist_csf_ids [PR.IP-01], sp80053_ids [CM-02], legacy_sha_ids []`; `audit-log-retention -> nist_csf_ids [DE.AE-03], sp80053_ids [AU-11], legacy_sha_ids []`; `remote-access-review -> nist_csf_ids [PR.AA-01], sp80053_ids [AC-17], legacy_sha_ids []`. DISA and CISA/NSA controls use empty mapping arrays except where explicitly listed above.

Curated starter controls use pinned provenance locators only: Microsoft `Account Policies / Password history`, `Account Policies / Minimum password length`, `Account Policies / Account lockout threshold`; CIS `Network / SMB signing`, `Network / SMBv1`, `Accounts / Guest account`; NIST `PR.IP-01`, `DE.AE-03`, `PR.AA-01`; DISA `WN22-SO-000025`, `WN22-SO-000315`, `WN11-CC-000390`; CISA/NSA `SSH root login`, `SSH password authentication`, `SSH allowlists`. Summary strings must stay concise and semantically aligned to the pinned title and locator.

Global duplicate handling is explicit: duplicate `pack_id` values, duplicate `control_id` values across all curated packs, duplicate values inside `profiles` arrays, duplicate values inside mapping arrays, or curated control IDs that violate the pinned inventory must fail validation.
Duplicate `control_id` values are validation errors whether they occur inside one curated pack or across multiple curated packs.

Generated-file JSON formatting is fixed for byte-stable outputs: UTF-8, two-space indentation, object keys written in declared field order, one trailing newline, and no extra whitespace variability. Byte-stable formatting requirements apply only to the generated catalog file; curated starter pack files are validated semantically and never rewritten.

Generated catalog failure semantics are fixed: if `control-packs/catalog.json` already exists and validation fails, the file must remain byte-identical to its pre-run contents; if it does not yet exist and validation fails, it must not be created.

The generated catalog summary file is fixed to top-level fields `generated_at`, `pack_count`, `control_count`, and `packs`, where each `packs[]` object contains exactly `pack_id`, `source_family`, `source_name`, `source_version`, and `control_count` with no extra fields.
Empty `controls` arrays are invalid for curated starter packs and must fail validation before any pack-level union checks run.
The builder must emit `control-packs/catalog.json` with `packs[]` ordered in ascending `pack_id` order exactly.
Extra fields are forbidden in generated catalog summary objects and in nested `provenance` and `mappings` objects inside curated control definitions.

Pinned pack control counts for closeout are: Microsoft starter `3`, CIS starter `3`, NIST starter `3`, DISA starter `3`, and CISA/NSA starter `3`, for a total catalog `control_count` of `15` and `pack_count` of `5`.
`README.md` and `control-packs/README.md` must explain that this slice is curated, file-backed, local-only, and intentionally avoids live source fetching and legacy CSV import.

`dt --check` is non-blocking for task correctness in this slice: if the command is unavailable, missing, or broken, closeout still passes once the builder command and focused pytest command succeed, but the environment regression must be reported.
testing_strategy: Add focused backend tests for contract validation, deterministic catalog generation, duplicate-ID rejection, malformed curated JSON rejection, inventory validation, and semantic array ordering.
Verify the generated `control-packs/catalog.json` contains the pinned pack count, pinned total control count, deterministic ordering, and byte-stable output across repeated runs.

Run `uv run --directory backend pytest tests/test_source_pack_contracts.py tests/test_source_pack_builder.py -q`, rerun the builder command twice, and then run `dt --check` from the repo root as a secondary managed-workspace gate when available.

risks: If the normalized contract is vague, later agent and remediation tasks will encode incompatible assumptions about control metadata.
If catalog ordering is nondeterministic, later task automation and review diffs will be noisy and hard to trust.
If curated starter packs drift from pinned inventory or metadata, later SHAna workflows will reason over unstable control IDs.
If this slice tries to do full corpus ingestion now, autonomous execution will stall in source-specific cleanup work instead of shipping a trustworthy starter catalog.

subtasks.description: Freeze the curated starter-pack contract, source-of-truth rule, fixed input set, filesystem layout, and catalog output before generating data files.

steps.description: Create typed Python contract models for packs, controls, provenance, mappings, and catalog summaries.

spec.detail: The typed contract layer lives under `backend/app/source_packs/contracts.py`, and all curated JSON plus the generated catalog in this task must conform to those exact fields and enums.

steps.description: Lock the repo-local layout and source-of-truth rule to authoritative curated starter pack inputs plus a generated catalog output.

spec.detail: Do not introduce database persistence, API routes, or legacy CSV import in this slice; the authoritative artifacts are curated checked-in JSON starter packs plus the generated `control-packs/catalog.json`.

subtasks.description: Build the local starter-pack validator and deterministic catalog builder.

steps.description: Implement the catalog helpers under `backend/app/source_packs/` and `backend/scripts/build_source_catalog.py`.

spec.detail: The builder must validate the five curated starter pack JSON files in place, reject malformed inputs, freeze the pinned UTC timestamp `2026-04-18T00:00:00Z`, and write only `control-packs/catalog.json` in stable order.

steps.description: Validate pinned inventory, canonical metadata, and union rules while generating the catalog summary.

spec.detail: The builder must prove each pack uses the pinned canonical metadata, that per-pack `platforms` and `profiles` match the canonical union of contained controls, and that the generated catalog summary reflects the exact five-pack, fifteen-control starter inventory.

subtasks.description: Check in the curated starter source packs and the generated normalized catalog summary.

steps.description: Add curated starter JSON pack files for Microsoft, CIS, NIST, DISA, and CISA/NSA source families.

spec.detail: Each curated starter pack must contain exactly three controls with the pinned IDs, titles, metadata, mappings, and provenance described in the task constraints. Curated controls must already be sorted by `control_id` in the checked-in JSON.

steps.description: Generate and commit the top-level catalog summary from the curated starter packs.

spec.detail: `control-packs/catalog.json` must be generated by the builder, committed, and reproducible from repo-local curated inputs only.

subtasks.description: Lock the curated starter-catalog slice with focused tests, docs, and deterministic verification commands.

steps.description: Add focused tests for contract validation, catalog determinism, malformed input rejection, and pinned starter inventory.

spec.detail: Tests must prove the catalog contains exactly five packs and fifteen controls, that pack ordering is ascending by `pack_id`, that each curated pack contains exactly the pinned three-control inventory, that array ordering is canonical, that malformed or duplicate curated content fails fast, and that repeated builder runs produce byte-identical `control-packs/catalog.json` output. Tests must also assert the deterministic free-text formulas, exact `packs[]` ascending `pack_id` order, atomic unchanged-catalog behavior on failure, and nested extra-field rejection in `provenance` and `mappings`. Tests must also cover missing expected curated files, extra JSON file rejection, unsorted `controls` arrays, duplicate IDs inside a single pack, duplicate mapping values after trim, and failure when pack-level `platforms` or `profiles` do not equal the canonical union of contained controls.

steps.description: Update repo docs and run the pinned verification commands.

spec.detail: `README.md` and `control-packs/README.md` must explain the curated local-only source-pack workflow and note that legacy CSV import is deferred. Closeout requires the builder command run twice plus the focused pytest command. Run `dt --check` from the repo root when available, but if it is missing or broken because of concurrent SummitFlow tooling refactors, report that environment regression separately and do not treat it as a source-pack task failure.
